scenarios.opening_query: Airport security found something in my carry-on that I completely forgot was in there.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was my pistol — I have a permit at home, but 'firearm at a security checkpoint' is charged as a felony here plus the federal civil penalty.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was my pistol — permitted at home, but a checkpoint firearm is a felony charge here plus the federal penalty.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what the item was and how it's charged
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a forgotten pocketknife — a citation-level misdemeanor the DA usually diverts.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A forgotten pocketknife — citation-level, usually diverted.

scenarios.opening_query: My son's fraternity prank went sideways and the university is talking to prosecutors.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Local Ordinances || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DA passed — what issued is a city noise-and-nuisance ordinance citation, plus the university's own conduct process.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DA passed — it's a city ordinance citation plus the university conduct process.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It issued as felony criminal mischief — the 'prank' damaged the rival's field turf for $30,000.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Felony criminal mischief — the turf damage hit $30,000.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm charged in a bar parking-lot incident I barely remember, and the other guy's story grows every week.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Non-Capital Murder
scenarios.hidden_fact: He died of his head injury last week — the DA refiled it as manslaughter, and everything about my defense just changed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He died of the head injury last week — it's refiled as manslaughter now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the victim's death elevated the charge
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's fully recovered — it stands as misdemeanor Assault IV, ugly but bounded.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He recovered — it stands as Assault IV.
scenarios.vetting_note: Victim-outcome fact; the heaviest escalation in the family.

scenarios.opening_query: The pharmacy called the police over my prescription and now I have a court date.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > White Collar Crime
scenarios.hidden_fact: They charged forgery of a prescription — and the DA's letter says they're reviewing 'a pattern across pharmacies,' which my lawyer friend calls fraud-unit territory.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's prescription forgery — and the DA mentions reviewing 'a pattern across pharmacies.' Fraud-unit territory.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a multi-incident fraud theory is forming
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's one count over a smudged date my doctor already confirmed was his — a misdemeanor headed for dismissal.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: One count over a smudged date my doctor confirmed — headed for dismissal.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter was driving my car when her friends shoplifted, and somehow SHE'S charged too.
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's 16 — it's juvenile court, with an informal-adjustment offer that keeps her record clean if handled right.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's 16 — juvenile court, with an informal-adjustment offer that keeps her clean if handled right.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's 18 as of last month — adult misdemeanor theft-by-receiving, and her college applications hang on it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She turned 18 last month — adult court, with college applications hanging on it.

scenarios.opening_query: There's a warrant for my husband over 'theft of services' from a gym membership dispute of all things.
scenarios.hidden_fact: There's no warrant at all — the 'warrant division' caller is the gym's collection agency running a fear script; the county confirmed nothing exists, and the deceptive threats are the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's no warrant — the county confirmed it. The 'warrant division' calls are the gym's collector running a fear script.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the warrant is real or a collection scare tactic
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The warrant's real — he ignored a citation over the disputed charges, and quashing it plus the misdemeanor defense is the work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's real — he ignored a citation, so quashing it plus the defense is the work.
scenarios.vetting_note: Fake-warrant collection scam vs real FTA.
